description: File Upload Exploit targets applications that accept user-supplied files without sufficient validation of type, content, or execution context. An attacker uploads a malicious web shell disguised as a permitted file type, then browses to it to achieve server-side code execution.

mitre_attack.mapping_note: T1505.003 applies after an uploaded file is executable as a web shell. Benign or non-executable uploads do not map to that technique.

cvss_methodology.note: CVSS measures vulnerability severity. It does not prescribe SOC response deadlines.
cvss_methodology.migration_note: CVSS v4.0 is current, but its metrics and scoring are not one-to-one with v3.1. Existing scenario vectors remain v3.1 until individually re-assessed.

response_framework.name: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3
response_framework.note: Response events implement Detect, Respond, Recover, and continuous-improvement outcomes. Numeric exercise thresholds are local policy, not NIST requirements.

timing_model.note: The 900-second floor prevents zero-length and division-by-zero windows at CVSS 10.0. These values are simulation policy and must be calibrated with organizational risk and service objectives.

incident_response_rules.description: Alert triage started within the difficulty-adjusted MTTA threshold
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_investigation_started occurs after mtta_threshold_sec or is absent

incident_response_rules.description: Incident assessed and confirmed or formally dismissed
incident_response_rules.trigger: neither incident_confirmed nor dismissal_approved is present

incident_response_rules.description: Relevant evidence preserved before destructive containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: incident_confirmed is present but evidence_preserved is absent or occurs after containment_initiated
containment_initiated is absent or occurs after ttc_max_sec

incident_response_rules.description: Root cause or malicious artifact eradicated after containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: containment_succeeded is present but eradication_completed is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Recovery validated before normal operation resumed
incident_response_rules.trigger: eradication_completed is present but recovery_validated is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Lessons learned and improvement actions recorded
incident_response_rules.trigger: recovery_validated is present but lessons_learned_recorded is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Medium-or-higher alerts are not denied without documented approval
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_denied is present for severity medium or higher without a preceding dismissal_approved event

scoring.ttc_factor: 1 when containment succeeds by ttc_expected; linear decay to 0 at ttc_max; 0 when containment is absent or later than ttc_max

response_difficulty_bonus.condition: awarded only when containment_succeeded is present
response_difficulty_bonus.zero_delay_policy: A zero-second investigation delay uses a one-second denominator and therefore receives the capped bonus.

custom_rules.description: FIM file-added event in an upload directory and content inspection identifies executable server-side code
Canonical filename, detected MIME type, and file signature disagree in a way that permits server-side execution
A double-extension file contains executable code and the web-server handler configuration would execute it

wazuh.note: Custom entries are dataset detection specifications and must be implemented and tested in the deployed Wazuh manager before use. Stock rule descriptions and levels match the linked upstream ruleset.

scenarios.label: Authenticated PHP Web Shell Upload via MIME-Type Bypass
scenarios.description: Low-privilege authenticated user uploads a PHP web shell with a '.jpg' extension but PHP MIME type. The application validates extension only; web server executes the file. Full RCE achieved under web-process account. Scope unchanged.

attack_context.rationale: Attacker requires a valid low-privilege user account to reach the upload endpoint.

component_rationale.AC_L: Simple extension bypass; no special environmental conditions
component_rationale.PR_L: Low-privilege account required to access upload endpoint
component_rationale.S_U: Web shell execution scope confined to web-server process
component_rationale.C_H: Full filesystem read accessible to web process
component_rationale.I_H: Arbitrary write and code execution on server
component_rationale.A_H: Attacker can terminate processes or drop files that disrupt service

detection.rationale: PHP extension in upload directory generates Wazuh FIM alert on file creation; correlating upload HTTP request with subsequent GET request to shell path is straightforward but requires log joining. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100401 (Executable content created in an upload directory); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Executable content created in an upload directory

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100401 level 9 fired: Executable content created in an upload directory.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 500s, within 750s medium threshold. FIM event correlated with upload access log; PHP execution confirmed from HTTP response body containing 'uid=www-data'.
Analyst-2 confirmed active web shell: GET /uploads/shell.jpg?cmd=id returned server user. File content inspection confirmed PHP exec() wrapper.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 removed shell.jpg from uploads directory; web server reconfigured to deny PHP execution in /uploads/ (AddHandler removed); uploading user account suspended.

GET /uploads/shell.jpg returns 403. PHP execution disabled in uploads directory. Upload endpoint now enforces MIME content inspection in addition to extension check. TTC_actual=3800s < TTC_expected=4320s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

scenarios.label: Unauthenticated Web Shell via Double Extension — Containment Failure
scenarios.description: Unauthenticated attacker uploads a web shell using a null-byte double extension (shell.php%00.jpg) to bypass extension validation. The web shell executes and establishes a reverse shell. MTTA is breached and no containment occurs before TTC_max.

attack_context.rationale: Authentication requirement follows the endpoint access described by the scenario.

component_rationale.AC_H: Null-byte injection technique requires specific server-side language behaviour (PHP < 5.3.4)
component_rationale.PR_N: No authentication required; upload endpoint publicly accessible
component_rationale.S_U: Web shell execution scope confined to web-server process
component_rationale.C_H: Full filesystem read accessible to web process
component_rationale.I_H: Arbitrary write and code execution; reverse shell established
component_rationale.A_H: Attacker can disrupt service by overwriting critical web application files

detection.rationale: Null-byte encoding in filename is not visible in standard access log display; FIM alert fires on file creation but the filename appears as a JPEG; only raw byte inspection reveals the embedded PHP extension. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100402 (Filename and content disagree after canonicalization); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Filename and content disagree after canonicalization

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100402 level 9 fired: Filename and content disagree after canonicalization.

Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 1050s, exceeds 900s high threshold. FUP-R01 triggered. Analyst reviewed FIM alert but filename appeared as shell.jpg in log display; PHP content missed at first inspection.

Analyst-2 confirmed PHP web shell via raw file inspection: null byte in filename truncated display; file executed by PHP interpreter. Reverse shell connection to attacker C2 already established before investigation began.

scenarios.label: Authenticated Double-Extension Bypass — Very-High Difficulty, Good Response
scenarios.description: Authenticated low-privilege user uploads a web shell using a .php.jpg double extension to bypass MIME validation without null bytes. High attack complexity (WAF partial block). Scope unchanged, limited integrity impact. Despite very-high detection difficulty the SOC contains within the expected window.

attack_context.rationale: Low-privilege user session required for the upload endpoint.

component_rationale.AC_H: Double-extension technique requires knowing web server AddHandler configuration
component_rationale.PR_L: Low-privilege authenticated user required to reach upload endpoint
component_rationale.S_U: Web shell execution confined to web-server process
component_rationale.C_H: Full filesystem read accessible to web process account
component_rationale.I_L: Limited write: attacker can create files in upload directory only

detection.rationale: A double-extension filename such as 'shell.php.jpg' may evade filename-only monitoring. Detection requires content inspection plus validation of the web server's handler configuration. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100403 (Executable content in a double-extension upload); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Executable content in a double-extension upload

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100403 level 8 fired: Executable content in a double-extension upload.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 1100s, within 1200s very-high threshold. Content-inspection log confirmed PHP inside shell.php.jpg. Accessed GET /uploads/shell.php.jpg — returned PHP execution output.
Analyst-2 confirmed web shell active; executed 'ls /var/www/html' via shell. Shell.php.jpg identified as persistent webshell; attacker has read access to application source code and config.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 removed shell.php.jpg; disabled .htaccess AddHandler override; restricted uploads to image/jpeg and image/png MIME only; deploying file content-inspection as mandatory upload gate.
Re-upload of test PHP file rejected with 415 Unsupported Media Type. Web shell removed and no longer accessible. TTC_actual=13000s < TTC_expected=14760s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

references.title: FIRST CVSS v4.0 Specification (current migration target)

dataset_metadata.source_policy: Use upstream standards and vendor rulesets; label project-specific detection logic as custom.
dataset_metadata.cvss_profile: Validated CVSS v3.1 compatibility profile; migrate through a separate expert review rather than automatic vector conversion.
